nameMeanings.name: Woodward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a forester employed to look after the trees and game in a forest, Middle English woodward (from Old English wudu ‘wood’ + weard ‘guardian, protector’).

History: Henry Woodward emigrated from England in 1635 and settled first in Dorchester, MA, and subsequently in Northampton, MA. He had many prominent descendants. Another Henry Woodward, born c. 1646 in the British West Indies, was the first English settler in SC (1664).

variants.name: Woodard
meanings: from the Norman Old French personal name Odard, Udard, Middle English Wodard, Wudard. It derives from the ancient Germanic name Authard, Othard, Odard, composed of the elements aud- ‘riches' + hard- ‘hard, bold’.
occupational name from Middle English wode ‘wood’ + herde ‘herd’ (Old English wudu + hierde), for a person who tended animals pastured in woodland.

variants.name: Woodworth
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Woodward or Wadworth, the latter being a habitational name from Wadworth in Yorkshire, which is derived from the Old English personal name Wada, Wadda + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Woodman
meanings: occupational name for a woodcutter or a forester (compare Woodward ), from Middle English wodeman ‘woodman’. In some instances this may be a variant of Wadman .
from the Middle English personal name Wudemann (Old English Wuduman, from wudu ‘wood’ + mann ‘man’).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Woodward was born in 1330, in Tamworth, Staffordshire, England. She married Robertus de Fulwood about 1355, in Tanworth in Arden, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1370, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 40.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Woodward(은)는 1330년 영국 스태퍼드실르 타워스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1355년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 탠워스 인 아든에서 Robertus de Fulwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Woodward родилась в 1330 г. в местности тамворт, стаффордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robertus de Fulwood около 1355 г. в местности танворт ин арден, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына.
